description:
Accessible instrument tuner for NVDA with reference tone playback for over two dozen instruments and tunings.
Live microphone tuning: press L to listen while you play a string. Every 2 seconds it reports one clear reading: in tune, sharp by a percentage, or flat by a percentage (100% equals one semitone). Press C to switch to chromatic mode, which detects the nearest note automatically instead of comparing against a specific selected string.
When a reading is exactly in tune, it plays a confirmation beep at the actual target note's pitch, then announces the note name. Press O to turn this beep on or off if you only want the spoken reading. Press R to repeat the last reading if you didn't catch it clearly. While live listening is on, Space cancels an in-progress beep and announcement immediately.
Settings (A4 reference pitch, volume, tone length, gap, sample rate, chromatic mode, beep length, beep on/off, microphone device, last instrument and tuning) persist across NVDA restarts.
changelog:
2.5.3: manually tested end-to-end on a real NVDA + Windows machine (the previous release had only been developed and unit tested in a sandbox with no Windows, NVDA, or real audio hardware). Confirmed working: dialog UI and reference tone playback, live microphone tuning and chromatic mode, the L/C/O/R shortcuts, microphone device switching, microphone-disconnect detection, the Advanced settings dialog, settings persistence across NVDA restarts, and Thai/English announcements. Updated the in-code "not yet tested" notes in __init__.py, mic_capture.py, and live_tuner.py, and the readme, to reflect this. No functional code changes in this release.
2.5.2: standards-compliance pass. Fixed the manifest name field (previously "Universal Tuner" with a space, which does not follow the required camelCase, no-space add-on identifier convention - now "universalTuner"). Split the version history out of the description field into this dedicated changelog field. Added author contact email, docFileName, and a bundled readme.html (previously pressing Help on this add-on in the Add-ons Manager had nothing to open). Added a GPL v2 COPYING.txt license file. Corrected lastTestedNVDAVersion, which had been left at a placeholder value (2099.1) that does not correspond to any real NVDA release. Added proper gettext-based translation support (locale/th) and fixed a bug where the live-tuner's spoken in-tune/sharp/flat readings were always announced in English regardless of NVDA's configured language - they now follow NVDA's language setting automatically like the rest of NVDA's own messages. The manual F1 Thai/English shortcuts-guide toggle is unchanged and still available on request in either language regardless of NVDA's configured language.
2.5.1: reordered Advanced settings to Sample rate, A4 reference, Gap between repeats, Beep length, Tone length, Microphone device, and added a "Tone length" field there (previously only adjustable from the main window with the plus/minus keys) - both per user testing feedback. Raised the default Gap between repeats to 2.0s, Beep length to 5.0s, and Tone length to 5.0s (previously 0.15s, 0.6s, and 2.0s) per user request following real-world testing. Replaced the pitch detection engine (normalized autocorrelation) with the YIN algorithm for live microphone tuning - measurably more accurate in testing, especially on the low guitar strings (E2/A2), with no dependency on microphone quality, so it benefits cheap and expensive microphones alike.
2.5.0: added a Microphone device field in Advanced settings, for users with more than one recording device connected - previously live listening always used whatever Windows treated as the default. Live listening now also detects if the microphone unexpectedly disconnects or stops responding, automatically stops itself, and announces this instead of silently continuing to show "Live listening: on" with nothing actually being analysed.
2.4.3: code review pass - fixed a latent bug where, if Beep length was set close to or longer than the ~2-second live-tuning report interval, an older round's delayed announcement could still fire after a newer one and speak stale information; fixed the one-shot beep/cue cache (used for the in-tune confirmation and the new number-key cue) not being size-capped like the reference-tone cache, which could let temp .wav files accumulate unbounded during a long session; removed several unused internal helpers left over from earlier revisions to keep the add-on lean.
2.4.2: fixed a regression from 2.4.0 where Space bar could no longer stop a reference tone started by a number key while live listening was on (you had to turn listening off first). While live listening is on, number keys now play the target note once instead of looping it - number keys during listening are for picking what to check the microphone against, not for tuning by ear - press the same or another number key to hear it again, or Space to cut it off early. If chromatic mode is also on, number keys no longer play any tone at all, since chromatic mode always compares against the nearest note regardless of which string is selected.
2.4.1: fixed the in-tune beep+announcement repeating every ~2 seconds while a note kept ringing or was held steady - it now announces once per attempt, and only announces again automatically once the reading actually changes or the sound stops and starts again; press R any time to hear the last reading again on demand. Shortened the spoken in-tune confirmation ("Note X. In tune.") by removing a redundant repeated word. Raised the "Gap between repeats" ceiling from 2 to 5 seconds to match the new beep length field.
2.4.0: shortened the live-tuning report interval from 2.5 to 2 seconds. Added an adjustable beep length setting and an O shortcut to disable the in-tune beep entirely. Added an R shortcut to repeat the last live tuning reading. Space bar now cancels an in-progress in-tune beep and spoken confirmation while live listening is on. Added a Reset to defaults button in Advanced settings. All new settings (beep length, beep on/off) now persist across NVDA restarts alongside the existing ones.
2.3.0: hardened for reliability after an independent code review and adversarial audio testing. Raised the confidence bar for accepting a microphone reading so that two strings ringing at once (bleed from an adjacent string) is far less likely to be confidently misreported - it now stays silent rather than guessing when the sound is ambiguous. Fixed a handle/resource leak if starting the microphone failed and was retried, fixed a crash risk when reopening the tuner window after closing it, and added thread-safety locking around the live-tuning analysis state.
Space bar, number-key string shortcuts, and the Selection list always agree on which note is currently playing.
